question.label: Landing check-in
question.name: What’s been most on your mind at work lately, Aisha?
question.description: Safe opener that lets her set the agenda before any friction is named.

answer: I am settling in, but I still feel like I am learning the unwritten rules in every meeting.

assessment.note: She is settling in but describes ongoing effort decoding 'unwritten rules in every meeting,' which signals mild drag on energy and confidence; also reveals a strong clarity issue worth probing next.

new_queue.label: Unwritten rules
new_queue.name: When you say 'unwritten rules,' what are you having to decode in meetings?

new_queue.label: Meeting uncertainty
new_queue.name: Which cross-functional meetings still feel hardest to step into?

new_queue.label: Decision rights map
new_queue.name: Where do the decision rights still feel fuzzy for you?

new_queue.label: Alignment friction
new_queue.name: What part of the alignment work is taking more effort than it should?

new_queue.label: Unspoken norms
new_queue.name: What are you still having to infer instead of just knowing?

new_queue.label: Manager boundaries
new_queue.name: What boundaries from me would help most right now?

history.answer_excerpt: I am settling in, but I still feel like I am learning the unwritten rules in every meeting.
I am settling in, but I still feel like I am learning the unwritten rules in every meeting.
